human_use.rationale: The Artifact is a human-operated mobile checkout. Its success depends on discoverability, navigation, input burden, error recovery, feedback, accessibility, touch ergonomics, interruption recovery, perceived latency, destructive-action safety, and cognitive load across cross-disability use.

objective_gate_note: All seven objective gates pass per generated/judging/blind-loop-01/candidate-a/evidence/objective-report.json. Qualitative human-use scores are not objective gates and are not used to override them.

lens_findings.discoverability: H1 'Checkout', banner disclosing synthetic scope, and 4 numbered task cards each with a live 'Incomplete/Complete' badge in the top-right corner (visible on initial screenshot). Users can see at a glance which section is done. However, there is no progress indicator, no section-nav, and no persistent visible order summary — the summary + Place order live only at the bottom of the page after all four task cards, so a user must know to scroll to complete the flow. Evidence: screenshots/candidate-a/01-initial-390x844.png; transcript audit landmarks/headings extra (nav:0, aside:0).
lens_findings.navigation: One long continuous form; no skip-link (transcript 'audit landmarks/headings' extra shows skipLinks: []); Edit contact/address/shipping/payment buttons live in the bottom summary and focus the first input of the target section. First Tab focus lands on the contact-name input (transcript 'keyboard: first Tab focus' -> INPUT#contact-name). Keyboard users must Tab through every focusable to reach Place order.
lens_findings.input_burden: Autocomplete tokens are present on all inputs, which helps AT autofill. Validation is permissive ('Please enter a value' for empty fields, checks only for '@' in email). Section-level Complete/Incomplete badges give per-section progress. Down-side: card-name (payment info) is persisted to localStorage between reloads (transcript 'reload persistence check' extra shows payment.card-name = 'Alex Morgan' — the code excludes card-number/expiry/security but not card-name), so users returning after interruption see a partial payment leftover.
lens_findings.error_prevention_recovery: Error summary at the top with h2 'There is a problem' and per-field links that focus the field on click. aria-invalid + aria-describedby are wired on invalid inputs. Messages are generic ('Please enter a value', 'Please enter a valid email address') rather than field-specific — less actionable than candidates B or C. On invalid submit the error summary receives focus and the assertive status region announces 'There are errors in the form. Please correct them to proceed.' Evidence: transcript 'post-invalid-submit report' errorSummaryVisible=true.
lens_findings.feedback_status: Status region is visually-hidden with aria-live='assertive'. Using assertive for routine transitions ('Processing your order...', 'Order successfully placed.') is stronger than necessary and interrupts screen-reader users; polite would be the appropriate level per WCAG guidance. No aria-live coupling on the Complete/Incomplete badges so screen-reader users get no announcement when a section becomes complete. Evidence: candidate-a/index.html line 17, candidate-a/app.js lines 122–124 and 240–260.
lens_findings.accessibility: Native semantics: fieldset/legend, labels-for, aria-invalid, error summary with role='alert' and tabindex=-1 that receives focus. H1 + numbered H2s. No landmark <aside>/<nav>, no skip-link. aria-live='assertive' on status region is misused for routine updates. Payment leakage on reload (see input_burden) is a soft privacy concern. Card-name field carries autocomplete='cc-name' but reload restore uses localStorage rather than browser autofill — this bypasses the user's own storage preferences.
lens_findings.responsive_touch_ergonomics: At 320x568 no horizontal scroll (transcript '320x568: viewport initial' hasHorizontalScroll=false). Buttons and inputs meet the mobile-touch objective gate. The Edit-{Contact/Address/Shipping/Payment} buttons in the review section are small and closely spaced 2x2, which is tighter than the corresponding controls in candidates B and C.
lens_findings.interruption_resumption: Auto-persists on every input; restores on reload — but card-name is restored too (transcript 'reload persistence check' extra payment.card-name = 'Alex Morgan'), which is a payment-field leak. No user-visible Save or Clear controls, no aria-live announcement on restore, and no explicit statement to the user about what will or will not persist.
lens_findings.latency_perception: Placement setTimeout is 500 ms (hard-coded slow) except under prefers-reduced-motion where it is 0 (candidate-a/app.js lines 250–260). aria-busy is set on the checkout root and status announces 'Processing your order...', but the delay is arbitrarily long for a purely local synthetic operation and the confirmation region only receives focus after the timeout. During my run, the confirmation was still not visible when I sampled 500 ms after click (transcript 'confirmation state' extra confirmationVisible=false, confirmationId='' at the sample instant); it did appear before I sampled again in the duplicate-safety report.
lens_findings.destructive_actions: Duplicate-submit safety is weak. Place order button is disabled only inside the 500 ms setTimeout callback — during that window, additional clicks or Enter presses schedule additional setTimeouts that all set the confirmation ID and re-focus the confirmation region. In practice a single SYN-2048 rendered, but there is no explicit placementStarted flag guarding submit, unlike candidate B. There is no user-facing destructive control (no Clear progress), so users cannot deliberately reset — this is safer for accidental data loss but leaves the persisted card-name until submit clears storage.
lens_findings.cognitive_load: Four task cards + bottom summary is a reasonable IA, and the per-section Incomplete/Complete badges are helpful for sighted users. However the absence of a persistent visible order summary or progress indicator, plus the merging of 'Order Summary and Review' into one bottom block (which also contains Edit buttons, the confirmation checkbox and Place order), forces users to hold more state in working memory. Evidence: screenshots/candidate-a/01-initial-390x844.png; screenshots/candidate-a/05-review-filled.png.

strengths: Per-section Incomplete/Complete badge is a clear sighted-user progress cue.
Reduced-motion path completes without delay (setTimeout 0 under prefers-reduced-motion).
Section semantics + fieldset/legend + labels + error summary meet the accessibility gate baseline.
Edit-{section} buttons in the review block focus the first input of the correct task card.

defects.finding: Payment field card-name is persisted to localStorage across reloads (transcript 'reload persistence check' extra payment.card-name = 'Alex Morgan'). The code excludes only card-number/expiry/security. Cardholder name is payment data and should not be persisted.
No placementStarted guard; Place order button is disabled only after the 500 ms setTimeout resolves, so rapid re-clicks during the busy window queue additional setTimeouts.

Status region is aria-live='assertive' for routine transitions (should be polite); Complete/Incomplete badges are not in a live region so screen-reader users get no per-section completion announcement.
Hard-coded 500 ms placement delay for a purely local synthetic confirmation with no meaningful interim feedback beyond a hidden aria-busy.
Field error messages default to generic 'Please enter a value' rather than field-specific guidance.
No skip-link, no section-nav, no progress indicator, and no persistent order summary — the summary + Place order live only at the bottom of the page.

uncertainty: Blinded evidence; Track paradigm inferred from artifact behaviour (task cards with completion badges).

Confirmation visibility at 500 ms was not visible when sampled by Playwright but did render before the duplicate-safety sample; end-user perceived latency may be shorter than described but is still noticeably higher than the 120–180 ms of the other candidates.

loop_02_recommendation.change: Exclude card-name from the persistence allowlist and add a placementStarted flag that disables the submit path immediately on the first click of Place order (not inside the setTimeout callback); also reduce the non-reduced-motion setTimeout from 500 ms to ≈150 ms and change the status region aria-live from 'assertive' to 'polite' for routine flow transitions.
loop_02_recommendation.evidence_ref: generated/judging/blind-loop-01/candidate-a/app.js lines 17 and 42 (persist filter), lines 179–260 (submit + timeout), and generated/judging/loop-01/judge-human-use/navigation-transcript-a.json entry 'reload persistence check'
